Title: Jasmonate promotes artemisinin biosynthesis by activating the TCP14-ORA complex in Artemisia annua

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for formation of the artemisinin precursor pool
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher DBR2 expression is associated with increased artemisinin biosynthesis; DBR2 promoter activation by AaTCP14/AaORA enhances artemisinin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DBR2 is a direct transcriptional target of AaTCP14 and the AaTCP14-AaORA complex. 2. AaTCP14 binds the D2 TCP-binding motif in the DBR2 promoter and activates its transcription. 3. AaORA synergistically enhances DBR2 promoter activation with AaTCP14, while AaJAZ8 represses this activation until relieved by MeJA.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA treatment (50–100 μM) enhances DBR2 promoter activation and artemisinin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: dual-LUC promoter activation assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; transgenic overexpression/antisense of AaTCP14 and AaORA; qRT-PCR; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoter activated by AaTCP14, AaORA, and more strongly by their combination; activation enhanced by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a reduction step essential for conversion of artemisinin pathway intermediates toward dihydroartemisinic acid-derived products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study focuses on transcriptional regulation of DBR2 rather than direct biochemical assay of DBR2 catalysis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: DBR2 is a key downstream target whose activation via AaTCP14/AaORA engineering may boost artemisinin yield.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point supported by promoter-centered regulation in this study
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native DBR2 promoter (reporter); 35S promoter for effector expression in transient assays

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for formation of the immediate artemisinin precursor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher ALDH1 expression is associated with increased artemisinin biosynthesis; ALDH1 promoter activation by AaTCP14/AaORA contributes to elevated artemisinin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ALDH1 is directly activated by AaTCP14 and synergistically by AaTCP14 plus AaORA. 2. AaTCP14 binds the A1 TCP-binding motif in the ALDH1 promoter. 3. ALDH1 expression rises in AaTCP14 and AaTCP14-AaORA overexpression lines and falls in AaTCP14 antisense plants.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA treatment enhances ALDH1 promoter activation by AaTCP14 and AaORA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: dual-LUC promoter activation assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; transgenic overexpression/antisense; qRT-PCR; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoter activity induced by AaTCP14 and AaORA, strongest under coexpression and MeJA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes aldehyde oxidation in the artemisinin pathway leading toward artemisinic acid/dihydroartemisinic acid-derived end products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes transcriptional regulation of ALDH1 rather than direct enzymology.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: ALDH1 expression can be enhanced together with AaTCP14/AaORA to improve artemisinin production.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native ALDH1 promoter (reporter); 35S promoter for effector expression in transient assays

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I TCP transcription factor; TCP domain
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator that increases artemisinin and DHAA content and decreases AA when overexpressed; antisense suppression reduces artemisinin production.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates DBR2 and ALDH1 promoters by binding TCP-binding motifs; indirectly up-regulates ADS, CYP71AV1, AaORA, AaWRKY1, AaMYC2, AaGSW1, AaAOC, and AaOPR3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: A trichome-enriched, JA-responsive activator that directly controls key late artemisinin biosynthetic genes and forms a synergistic complex with AaORA.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms the AaTCP14-AaORA transcriptional activator complex; can be incorporated into an AaJAZ8-AaTCP14-AaORA repressive complex context
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Potential positive feedback with JA biosynthesis/signaling inferred from up-regulation of AaAOC and AaOPR3 in AaTCP14 overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA treatment induces AaTCP14 expression and enhances its promoter activation effects
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in young leaves and trichomes; decreases during leaf development; induced by MeJA, peaking at 9 h
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; GST pull-down; BiFC; co-IP; subcellular localization; qRT-PCR; GUS reporter; overexpression and antisense transgenics; dual-LUC; Y1H; EMSA; HPLC metabolite analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaTCP14 physically interacts with AaORA in vitro and in vivo. 2. AaTCP14 is trichome-enriched, nucleus-localized, and induced by MeJA. 3. AaTCP14 directly binds and activates DBR2 and ALDH1 promoters. 4. Overexpression of AaTCP14 increases artemisinin accumulation, whereas antisense suppression decreases it. 5. AaTCP14 acts synergistically with AaORA, and this activation is antagonized by AaJAZ8.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter in transient assays; native AaTCP14 promoter in GUS analysis
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious morphological differences observed in overexpression or antisense lines under tested conditions
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AaTCP14 uses its C-terminal domain to interact with both AaORA and AaJAZ8; AaORA does not alter AaTCP14 DNA-binding ability in vitro.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to increase artemisinin yield, especially in combination with AaORA or with strategies relieving AaJAZ8 repression.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF transcription factor; APETALA2/ETHYLENE-RESPONSE FACTOR family
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator that promotes artemisinin biosynthesis, partly through synergy with AaTCP14.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation via protein-complex-assisted promoter activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates promoters of ADS, CYP71AV1, DBR2, and ALDH1; synergistically enhances DBR2 and ALDH1 activation with AaTCP14
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key JA-responsive trichome-specific activator whose function is partly dependent on AaTCP14 and is released from repression by AaJAZ8.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: MeJA/jasmonate; AaGSW1 and likely AaMYC2 pathway as discussed
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms AaTCP14-AaORA activator complex; targeted by AaJAZ8 which disrupts the complex
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in JA-responsive feedforward regulation of artemisinin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA induces AaORA expression and enhances promoter activation by AaORA-containing complexes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Trichome-specific/young leaf-associated according to paper; induced by MeJA with peak at 9 h
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: dual-LUC; Y2H; BiFC; co-IP; transgenic overexpression with or without AaTCP14 antisense; protoplast transactivation assays; qRT-PCR; HPLC
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaORA interacts directly with AaTCP14 and synergistically activates DBR2 and ALDH1. 2. AaORA-mediated activation of DBR2 and ALDH1 is partly dependent on AaTCP14. 3. AaORA is induced by MeJA and targeted by AaJAZ8 repression. 4. Co-overexpression of AaORA and AaTCP14 yields higher artemisinin than AaTCP14 overexpression alone.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter in transient assays and effectors
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AaORA can also activate ADS and CYP71AV1 independently of AaTCP14; its C terminus mediates interaction with AaTCP14, whereas both N- and C-terminal regions can interact with AaJAZ8.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful positive regulator for engineering higher artemisinin, especially in combination with AaTCP14.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JAZ repressor protein; ZIM domain; Jas domain
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator that represses artemisinin biosynthesis; overexpression lowers artemisinin and reduces ADS, CYP71AV1, DBR2, and ALDH1 expression.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein-protein interaction–mediated repression; disruption of activator complex; degradation-dependent derepression under JA
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly represses DBR2 promoter activation by inhibiting AaTCP14, AaORA, and their complex
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as the JA-removable brake on artemisinin biosynthesis by binding both AaTCP14 and AaORA and interfering with their activator complex.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: MeJA/jasmonate; COI1-mediated JA perception; 26S proteasome degradation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Associates with AaTCP14 and AaORA and relocates/restricts the complex in nuclear bodies; forms repressive AaJAZ8-AaTCP14-AaORA complex context
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: MeJA induces AaJAZ8 transcript but also promotes its protein degradation, creating JA-responsive derepression/homeostatic control
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA induces transcript accumulation and triggers 26S proteasome-mediated protein degradation; MG132 blocks degradation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broadly expressed, highest in trichomes; induced by MeJA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression and dominant AaJAZ8Δjas lines; qRT-PCR; HPLC; Y2H; BiFC; colocalization; LUC complementation; co-IP; Y3H; competitive LUC assay; MeJA/MG132 degradation assay; dual-LUC repression assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaJAZ8 is a JA-responsive repressor that lowers artemisinin biosynthesis. 2. AaJAZ8 physically interacts with both AaTCP14 and AaORA. 3. AaJAZ8 competitively attenuates AaTCP14-AaORA interaction and suppresses DBR2 promoter activation. 4. MeJA-triggered degradation of AaJAZ8 relieves this repression and permits stronger artemisinin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter in transient assays; MET25 promoter in Y3H bridge expression
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only AaJAZ8 among tested AaJAZ proteins interacted with AaTCP14, whereas AaORA interacted with multiple AaJAZs; ZIM domain is required for interaction with AaTCP14 and AaORA.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing AaJAZ8 activity or preventing its accumulation could enhance artemisinin production, but JA signaling pleiotropy should be considered.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential signaling pleiotropy is implied, but no explicit whole-plant penalty was reported for the manipulations tested.
